intro.uz: Emotional Mastery 2 — hissiyotni tana orqali emas, mental fokus orqali boshqarish. AJ tashvish (anxiety) sizni sekinlashtirishini, yakuniy natijaga va kuchli sabablarga eʼtibor qaratish esa tez oʻrgatishini tushuntiradi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi shu gʻoyaning tili: maslahat modallari (should / ought to / had better / why don't you) va nol hamda birinchi shart (real sabab-natija).
intro.en: Emotional Mastery 2 — controlling your emotions through mental focus, not just your body. AJ explains that anxiety slows you down, while focusing on the end result and compelling reasons speeds you up. This week's two grammar topics are the language of that idea: modals of advice (should / ought to / had better / why don't you) and the zero & first conditional (real cause and effect).

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida oʻzingizga va boshqalarga toʻxtamasdan maslahat bera olasiz (You should… / You'd better…) va haqiqiy sabab-natijani toʻgʻri shakllantira olasiz (If I…, I will…).
canDo.en: By the end you can give advice to yourself and others without pausing (You should… / You'd better…) and form real cause-and-effect correctly (If I…, I will…).

grammar.titleUz: Maslahat modallari: should / ought to / had better / why don't you
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 3 — give and justify advice and recommendations
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can give advice and make suggestions using should / had better
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida maslahatni “-sang yaxshi”, “-ing kerak” yoki “nega ...maysan?” bilan beramiz. Ingliz tili buni bitta modal soʻz bilan hal qiladi va undan keyingi feʼlni oʻzgartirmaydi: should/had better dan keyin feʼl sof shaklda (to va -s siz), faqat ought va have to dan keyin “to” keladi. “Why don't you…?” savol koʻrinishida boʻlsa ham, aslida taklif/maslahat.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ You should to focus on the result. He should focuses. ✅ You should focus on the result. He should focus. Sababi: should / had better / can — bularning barchasi feʼlni sof (to-siz, -s-siz) shaklda talab qiladi; faqat ought “to” bilan keladi (ought to focus).

examples.en: You should focus on the end result, not on tests.
examples.uz: Testga emas, yakuniy natijaga eʼtibor bering.

examples.en: You'd better relax before you study.
Why don't you write down your reasons for learning English?
examples.uz: Ingliz tili oʻrganish sabablaringizni yozib qoʻysangiz-chi?

exercises.prompt: You feel anxious? You ___ focus on the end result, not the problems.
exercises.hintUz: should + sof feʼl (to va -s qoʻshilmaydi): should focus

exercises.prompt: The exam is tomorrow. You'd ___ get some sleep tonight.
exercises.hintUz: had better + sof feʼl: you'd better get (ogohlantiruvchi maslahat)

exercises.prompt: ___ you visualize your goal every morning?

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingizga uchta maslahat ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “I should … I shouldn't … I'd better … .”
exercises.hintUz: modaldan keyin feʼl sof shaklda (to va -s siz)

grammar.titleUz: Nol va birinchi shart: haqiqiy sabab-natija
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 & 3 — talk about real situations and their results
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can talk about real conditions and their likely results (if + present, will)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida ikkala jumla qismi ham kelasi zamonda kelishi mumkin: “Mashq qilsang, yaxshilanadi”. Ingliz tilida esa “if” dan keyin hech qachon “will” ishlatilmaydi — u yerda present simple turadi; “will” faqat natija qismida keladi. Nol shartda (doimiy haqiqat) ikkala tomon ham present simple.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ If you will practise, your English will improve. ✅ If you practise, your English will improve. Qoida: “ikkita will boʻlmaydi” — “if” qismida present simple, natija qismida will.

examples.en: If I feel anxious, I learn slowly.
If you focus on the end result, you will feel motivated.
If a robber breaks into the house, I will call 911.
examples.uz: Uyga oʻgʻri kirsa, 911 ga qoʻngʻiroq qilaman.

exercises.hintUz: doimiy haqiqat = nol shart: present + present (boils)

exercises.prompt: If you practise every day, your English ___.
exercises.hintUz: kelajak ehtimoli = birinchi shart: natija qismida will + feʼl

exercises.prompt: If the fire alarm ___ off, we will leave the building.
exercises.hintUz: “if” qismida present simple (goes) — bu yerda will ishlatilmaydi

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda ikkita jumla ayting: bittasi nol shart (“If I …, I …”), bittasi birinchi shart (“If I …, I will …”).
exercises.hintUz: “if” qismida doim present simple; will faqat natijada

vocab.defEn: getting a language and keeping it, so you don't forget it
vocab.example: Anxiety is the number one negative factor in language acquisition.

vocab.defEn: a feeling of nervousness or stress
vocab.example: Anxiety makes you learn more slowly.

vocab.defEn: to feel worried, nervous or stressed
vocab.example: I feel anxious before a big test.

vocab.defEn: to see something in your mind; to imagine it
vocab.example: Every day he visualizes arriving in New York.
Relaxed students have better listening comprehension.

vocab.uz: eʼtibor qaratmoq, diqqatni bir narsaga jamlash
vocab.defEn: to give your attention to one thing
vocab.example: Focus on the end result, not on your mistakes.

vocab.defEn: the final outcome you want to reach
vocab.example: Focus on the end result every day.

vocab.defEn: strong, inspiring reasons that make you want to act
vocab.example: You need compelling reasons to learn English.
Ultimately, you must study every day.

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda oʻqituvchi bolalardan “qanday vaziyatda 911 ga qoʻngʻiroq qilasiz?” deb soʻraydi va bolalar “agar ... boʻlsa” deb javob beradi. Emotional Mastery mavzusiga mos: xavotir (anxiety) paytida ham tinch fikrlash muhim. Bolalar sanaydigan har bir javob — birinchi shart (grammatika B!). Sizningcha, ular yana qaysi favqulodda vaziyatlarni aytishi mumkin?
warmup.en: In this dialogue a teacher asks children when they would call 911, and they answer with “If…”. It fits the Emotional Mastery theme: staying calm and thinking clearly under stress. Every answer the children give is a first conditional (grammar B!). What other emergencies do you think they might name?

keyVocab.uz: chiroyli/maxsus kiyim kiymoq, boshqa qiyofaga kirmoq
keyVocab.defEn: to put on special or fancy clothes, or a disguise
an occupation that requires special training
a person who protects people from drowning
to press the buttons to make a telephone call
to enter a house illegally, especially by force
to ring or sound loudly, e.g. an alarm
a dangerous condition where the heart stops beating properly
a person who robs or steals

quiz.q: How many times a day does the average adult laugh?
quiz.qUz: Oʻrtacha katta odam kuniga necha marta kuladi?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: a) 17. Tim 70 dedi va xato qildi. Neil aytadiki, oʻrtacha katta odam kuniga atigi 17 marta kuladi — bolalar esa kuniga taxminan 300 marta! Demak, koʻproq kulishimiz kerak (you should laugh more — grammatika A!).

vocab.defEn: a loud laugh that you can't control
to laugh very loudly for a long time
to stop breathing because something is blocking your throat
the sheet of muscle separating your lungs from your stomach
having difficulty breathing; struggling for air

funEnglish.title: How to Make Stress Your Friend | Kelly McGonigal | TED

speakingPrompt.en: A friend feels anxious and stressed about learning English. What advice would you give them? Tell them what they should focus on, and say what will happen if they follow your advice.
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: maslahat modallari (You should… / You'd better… / Why don't you…?) va shart gaplar (If you focus on…, you will…).
